# Arrival — Launch Roadmap

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/gen-roadmap.mjs from docs/roadmap.html — do not hand-edit. -->

**The roadmap has three copies, and this is the reading order:**

| Copy | What it's for |
|---|---|
| [`docs/roadmap.html`](roadmap.html) | **The source.** The full interactive page — filters, state, PR links, copy-as-issue. Edit the `EPICS` array here; everything else is generated from it. |
| The live artifact | The published, shareable view of `docs/roadmap.html` (same file, republished in place — the URL never changes). See `reference-roadmap-artifact` in project memory. |
| This file | The plain-text mirror, for reading in the repo and in diffs. Run `node scripts/gen-roadmap.mjs` after editing the HTML; never hand-edit it. |

Tasks are numbered by epic prefix (D1, V3, B4…). This mirror is ordered
**low → high priority (the bottom ships first)**; the HTML page reorders to
ship-blocking-first, which is the better read. Every task is scoped as one PR.

> **The gate:** do not submit to the App Store before the live backend ships —
> a simulation-only build is a guideline 4.2 (minimal functionality) rejection.
> See `docs/REVIEW_NOTES.md`.

## D — Delight & depth (P3)

The layer that makes Arrival feel loved rather than shipped. None of it blocks launch; all of it builds retention. Pick these up between heavier PRs.

| # | Slice | What | Size | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **D1** | `feat/footprints-v2` | Pre-rendered shoe-print PNGs (per member color, both feet) as Marker image props — no live glyph views. *⏸ Blocked on YOUR device soak test (roadmap T2) — old-arch dev build must prove stable first* | M | Needs you |
| **D2** | `feat/recap-share-card` | Branded card (mark, session, earned stats, crew facepile) captured via view-shot; share from live recap or any archived session. *✦ Shipped — PR #12 merged (iOS shares the PNG; Android text fallback until expo-sharing)* | M | **Shipped** (PR #12 ) |
| **D3** | `feat/feed-reactions` | Long-press a timeline row to react (👍❤️😂🎉); chips stack on the row, first arriver auto-celebrates later arrivals. *✦ Shipped — PR #13 merged (demo-local; live replication shipped with B6, PR #23)* | M | **Shipped** (PR #13 ) |
| **D4** | `feat/live-activity` | Glanceable convoy status (ETAs, active stop) while the user navigates in another app. Config-plugin + widget extension. *⏸ Needs a dev-client/Xcode session — native widget extension can't ship from CI alone (eas.json is ready)* | L | In progress |
| **D5** | `feat/leader-mode` | Owner pins an ordered set of waypoints pre-trip; members see the shared itinerary and "follow the leader" camera. | L | Open |

## V — Vertical Truth — own the z-axis (P2)

Every competitor’s map is 2D and lies indoors, underground, and on mountains. No mainstream tracker answers "which floor?" — this is Arrival’s wedge. The demo badge UI shipped in PR #3; these wire the real sensors.

| # | Slice | What | Size | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **V1** | `feat/mall-scenario` | Hudson Yards food-court convergence from four floors, live level chips, scenario-integrity tests. *✦ Shipped — PR #7 merged* | M | **Shipped** (PR #7 ) |
| **V2** | `feat/baro-floor-engine` | Pressure on every position packet (CMAltimeter / TYPE_PRESSURE); cross-device bias calibration when members are co-located; per-member floor delta. Ships first — powers everything below. | M | Open |
| **V3** | `feat/floor-badge-live` | CLLocation.floor in venue-mapped buildings with floor-delta fallback; confidence-gated; desaturate pucks not on your floor. *✦ Demo badge UI merged (PR #3) + mall demo merged (PR #7); this task wires the real sensors — needs feat/baro-floor-engine* | S | In progress |
| **V4** | `feat/underground-state` | Fused detector (GPS accuracy collapse + baro descent + transit/parking POI proximity) pins the puck at last good fix: "entered P2 Garage, 3:41". Resurface event on fix recovery. | M | Open |
| **V5** | `feat/altitude-gated-arrivals` | Arrival confirmation requires horizontal geofence AND vertical agreement — kills the overpass / parking-deck false "arrived". | S | Open |
| **V6** | `feat/parked-car-pin` | Auto-drop a pin on the driving→walking activity transition; level from baro delta since garage entry; new parked_car stop kind with nav deep-link. | M | Open |
| **V7** | `feat/hike-mode` | Fused GPS+baro altitude per trail vertex: gain/loss stats, climb/descend badges, Tobler-adjusted ETAs, recap elevation profile, "400 ft above you" on member cards. | M | Open |

## G — Guardian Loop — kids & care (P2)

Converts a group toy into something a parent relies on. Detection runs on the kid’s device via OS primitives (geofences, activity recognition), not on a parent staring at a dot.

| # | Slice | What | Size | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **G1** | `feat/battery-telemetry` | Battery % on position packets and rail chips; pushes at 20/10%; at 5% a durable last-known snapshot (position, floor, heading, activity). Dark pucks carry reason codes: died / underground / unshared / no signal. | S | Open |
| **G2** | `feat/watch-zones` | Parent-drawn zone syncs to the kid’s device; OS geofencing (CLCircularRegion / GeofencingClient) fires exit/enter while backgrounded; push to parent, local notice to kid. | S | Open |
| **G3** | `feat/smart-checkin` | Parent ping → time-sensitive push with lock-screen "I’m OK" action; auto-trigger on odd dwell (stopped >N min at a non-place); 2-min no-ack escalates to max-rate sampling + status panel. | M | Open |
| **G4** | `feat/unexpected-ride-alert` | Activity recognition detects a kid member entering a vehicle with no adult member co-moving within 30 m → high-priority push with the transition point pinned. Adult/kid roles on membership; expected-ride windows to mute it. *Only possible because sessions are group-scoped — single-user trackers can’t do this* | M | Open |
| **G5** | `feat/kid-sos` | Long-press-armed SOS + lock-screen widget: max sampling, DND-bypassing push to all adults, parent auto-follow mode, reassurance screen on the kid’s device; persists until a parent clears it. | S | Open |
| **G6** | `feat/pickup-choreography` | Promote any stop to a pickup point: kid-side geofence entry/exit events, "leave now" nudge timed to the kid’s walking ETA vs parent driving ETA, auto "reunited" on mutual geofence entry. | S | Open |
| **G7** | `feat/security-handoff-card` | Lost-kid moment: one-tap shareable image — photo, "wearing" note, last position + floor + heading + battery/underground context — via the OS share sheet; auto-arms SOS follow mode. | M | Open |

## C — Richer coordination (P2)

The features that make the map useful beyond watching dots move — finding places, getting told when things happen, and trusting ETAs.

| # | Slice | What | Size | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **C1** | `feat/session-archive` | Auto-archive on completion; Home list; read-only decluttered map with all traces; NYC walk pre-seeded. *✦ Shipped — PR #8 merged* | M | **Shipped** (PR #8 ) |
| **C2** | `feat/place-search` | Search-as-you-type for the destination, with result cards carrying a star rating, review count, distance, and a link out — a destination is a decision, not a pin. The sheet, the ranking and the provider seam shipped with free roam (lib/places, Place shaped to Yelp's Business Search response); OpenStreetMap fills it today, which knows where things are but nothing about what people think of them. *◐ Partly shipped — PR #43. REMAINING: Yelp behind a Supabase edge proxy (the key can never ship in the app). Swap the call inside searchPlaces and no UI changes.* | M | Open |
| **C3** | `feat/nav-deeplinks` | lib/nav-deeplinks.ts URL builders; Navigate action on destination, stops, and suggestions. *✦ Shipped — PR #2 merged (Navigate pill on stop cards + tappable destination)* | S | **Shipped** (PR #2 ) |
| **C4** | `feat/push-notifications` | expo-notifications + Expo Push: stop posted, suggestion confirmed, member arrived, session ending in 15 min. *Needs realtime backend (P1)* | M | Open |
| **C5** | `feat/arrival-geofences` | expo-location geofences on destination + confirmed stops; entering posts the feed event and flips member state. | M | Open |
| **C6** | `feat/eta-service` | Edge cron recomputes each moving member’s ETA every ~2 min with traffic; straight-line fallback offline. *Needs supabase-setup (P1)* | M | Open |
| **C7** | `feat/stale-members` | "Last seen 4m ago" badge past freshness thresholds; never silently drop a member from the map. | S | Open |
| **C8** | `feat/meet-time` | A destination without a time only says WHERE. With one, the app answers the question people actually open it for: not "12 minutes away" but "4 minutes early" — or "8 minutes late", which is the one that makes you text the group. trips.meet_at + a set_meet_time RPC any member can call; the header reads "Joe's Pizza · 8:00 PM" (separately tappable, because a group settles on eight o'clock long before it settles on the restaurant); and every ETA in the product becomes slack against the meeting. Cards, rail chips and puck tags all read "8 late", with the ETA demoted to the label beneath — and lateness is the ONLY thing that takes a member's colour away, because being early is not news. The group line becomes "Everyone's on time" or "Dan is 8 min late". The picker is a list of quarter-hours, not a date wheel: nobody arranges to meet at 7:23, and it keeps us off a native picker dependency, which on this project means no pod install, which means the fmt/Xcode-26 patch survives and nobody has to rebuild. *Branch feat/meet-time — awaiting your on-device pass. ⚠ Migration 0006 is NOT applied to the live project yet (needs your explicit go — demo mode works without it). THE TRAP IT AVOIDS: ends_at is what archives a session. Start a 30-minute session, agree to meet in four hours, and without set_meet_time pushing ends_at out, the session dies three hours before the thing everyone just agreed to — archiving them all mid-walk. Verified in scripts/verify-backend.mjs, including that the old end really was too early.* | M | Needs you |
| **C9** | `feat/secondary-locations` | One master location for the group; anyone may optionally add their own stops on the way ("grabbing coffee first"). A stop is NOT a second destination — it is the EXPLANATION for a number: adding a 15-minute coffee flips you from 5 early to 10 late, and the group sees why, instead of wondering why your puck is walking away from them. Your ETA routes through your unresolved stops; your card and puck tag read "via Think Coffee"; the stop auto-resolves within 40 m. DestinationSheet does double duty with a mode prop — same search, same rating cards, same Yelp seam. *MOSTLY ALREADY IN THE SCHEMA: `stops` has created_by, kind='announcement' (literally "I'm stopping here, FYI"), duration_min and resolved_at — the last two written by nothing today. This wires routing meaning into a model that already exists. Deliberately NOT building group waypoints: one master, N personal stops. Needs feat/meet-time to be worth much.* | M | Needs you |

## H — Honest Map — trust when GPS fails (P1)

A live map people rely on in exactly the moments GPS quits: honest uncertainty during dropouts, and phone-to-phone convergence for the last hundred feet.

| # | Slice | What | Size | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **H1** | `feat/backfill-staleness` | Ring buffer of samples during connectivity outages; batch upsert + trail splice on reconnect. Puck grows an uncertainty ring with age × last speed instead of sitting confidently wrong. *Prerequisite for underground/mountain trust; pairs with feat/underground-state* | S | Open |
| **H2** | `feat/last-100ft-finder` | Mutual "Find mode": session-scoped BLE advertise/scan → RSSI hot/cold proximity meter with baro floor hint; UWB tier (NISession / androidx.core.uwb) gives a distance + bearing arrow on capable phones. | L | Open |
| **H3** | `feat/direction-earned` | A phone rides in a back pocket, a bag, a swinging hand — so the compass (watchHeadingAsync) is meaningless: it reports where the DEVICE faces, which in a pocket is its owner's backside. We show course over ground (watchPositionAsync → coords.heading): the way the BODY travels, the same number however the phone is carried. Course has one honest property a compass lacks — WHEN YOU STOP, IT CEASES TO EXIST (iOS says so out loud: it reports -1). So a direction is now EARNED, by a gate every member goes through (lib/motion): hysteretic speed thresholds so the arrow cannot flicker at a crosswalk, a displacement floor so a stationary phone's GPS wander cannot fake motion, circular averaging (350° and 10° average to north — a plain mean says 180°, exactly backwards), and a 45-second hold across a pause before the course is finally dropped. Three states: MOVING (lit tip) · PAUSED (dimmed — we still know their course) · STILL (no arrow at all, and the absence is the information: they are not going anywhere). `heading` became `number ⎮ null`, which kills the old default of 0 — due north — a confident lie about anyone who had never moved. The demo now synthesizes noisy fixes (speed jitter, -1 course dropouts, junk accuracy) through the same gate, because an environment that cannot reproduce the bug cannot prove the fix: the Expo Go lesson in a new hat. *Branch feat/direction-earned — awaiting your on-device pass, then PR. MAP-CONTRACT CHANGE: the direction tip is now always mounted and hidden with opacity. It used to be a conditional child (`moving && …`), survivable only because `moving` almost never changed — the gate makes it change for real, and adding or removing a subview of a live annotation view is exactly the churn that drops pucks (#5911). Enforced by src/map-contract.test.ts.* | M | Needs you |

## B — Live backend (M1) — sim becomes real (P1)

The core promise: two phones see each other move. Everything in this epic replaces the simulation with Supabase while keeping the UI you already have.

| # | Slice | What | Size | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **B1** | `infra/supabase-setup` | SPEC §5.2 schema + RLS + join/create RPCs; CI applies migrations to real Postgres and smokes the RPCs on every PR. *✦ Shipped — PR #16 merged; live project migrated + verified* | M | **Shipped** (PR #16 ) |
| **B2** | `feat/session-crud` | Create mints a server-side join code via create_trip; Home joins by code via join_trip; demo fallback behind the config gate; verify-backend script proves the whole flow + RLS. *✦ Shipped — PR #17 merged; verify-backend green against the live project* | M | **Shipped** (PR #17 ) |
| **B3** | `feat/invite-links` | Join codes, arrival.app universal links, join/[code] preview screen, 12-member cap, owner lock + regenerate (F1a). *feat/session-crud* | L | Open |
| **B4** | `feat/realtime-positions` | Private trip:{id} broadcast channel (RLS-authorized), GPS publish @3s + 30s snapshots, roster/feed via postgres_changes, live trails/ETAs/steps; verify-backend proves a 2-user realtime round-trip (10/10). *✦ Shipped — PR #19 merged. YOUR acceptance test: two phones, one link, walk around* | L | **Shipped** (PR #19 ) |
| **B5** | `feat/background-tracking` | expo-task-manager background updates; speed-adaptive sampling and the stationary low-power downshift (§5.4). *feat/realtime-positions, needs dev client* | L | Open |
| **B6** | `feat/stops-live` | Stops/votes/joins/reactions over Postgres + postgres_changes; server triggers own the rules (creator auto-upvote, 2-vote confirm, feed events); toggle_reaction RPC. *✦ Shipped — PR #23 (17/17 live verification checks incl. confirm trigger + RLS negative)* | M | **Shipped** (PR #23 ) |

## S — App Store readiness (P0)

Location apps get extra review scrutiny. This epic is everything Apple and Google will actually check — none of it is optional, and half of it has lead time. Start the review-sensitive items early.

| **S6** | `infra/crash-telemetry` | Sentry (expo plugin) with source maps via EAS; release health dashboard before public TestFlight. | S | Open |

## T — Stability & correctness — ships first (P0)

You said it yourself: it needs to work flawlessly. This epic locks in the marker-stability work, puts tests around the map, and makes regressions impossible to miss. Highest priority on the page.

| # | Slice | What | Size | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **T1** | `test/unit-foundation` | jest-expo; logic extracted to lib/ modules (format, trail, clusters, convergence, lights, archive); 84 tests. *✦ Shipped — PR #6 merged (convention: every logic PR ships tests)* | M | **Shipped** (PR #6 ) |
| **T2** | `fix/marker-stability-validation` | Dev build (old arch) soak test: 15 min of trails-on zoom churn across both scenarios; document results; decide if PROVIDER_GOOGLE fallback flag is needed. *Watch react-native-maps #5911 / PR #5938 for the real fix* | S | Open |
| **T3** | `perf/render-pass` | Markers/rail chips/ring dots/feed rows/stop cards memoized on exactly what they draw; tick 250ms → 1s once everyone arrives. *✦ Shipped — PR #10 merged (on-device FPS profiling still worth a pass with your soak test)* | M | **Shipped** (PR #10 ) |
| **T4** | `test/e2e-smoke` | Flows: create session → trails toggle → select member → retrace → recap; runs on simulator in CI. | M | Open |
| **T5** | `infra/ci` | GitHub Actions: tsc --noEmit, eslint (flat config), jest — one job on every PR and main push. *✦ Shipped — PRs #4 + #9 merged (E2E lane lives in test/e2e-smoke)* | S | **Shipped** (PRs #4 + #9 ) |
| **T6** | `fix/error-boundaries` | Screen-level boundaries with recovery UI; defined empty/loading states for every surface before real network data arrives. *✦ Root boundary + recovery screen merged (PR #5); designed empty states remain* | S | In progress |

## F — Field-test fixes — everything the two-phone tests found (P0)

Work that came out of Paul testing on real hardware with two iPhones. Nothing here was on the plan; all of it was a real defect or a gap the demo hid. Kept as a numbered epic so the fixes are traceable to the PRs that shipped them.

| # | Slice | What | Size | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **F1** | `fix/live-roster-resilience` | Host saw "someone joined" but no puck. Layered delivery so no single realtime path failure blinds anyone: broadcast (3s) → fresher-wins snapshot merge (10s) → trip_members UPDATE subscription → 15s roster poll; unknown-sender recovery; send failures surfaced once. *✦ Shipped — PR #24* | M | **Shipped** (PR #24) |
| **F2** | `fix/select-zoom` | Tapping a chip while zoomed out added a duplicate puck instead of zooming. Apple Maps ignores camera.zoom — it needs altitude. Selected member no longer duplicates into the facepile. *✦ Shipped — PR #25* | S | **Shipped** (PR #25) |
| **F3** | `feat/invite-copy-link` | Big tappable join code, Copy-link primary with "Copied ✓" feedback (expo-clipboard), Share… secondary. *✦ Shipped — PR #26* | S | **Shipped** (PR #26) |
| **F4** | `fix/sheet-anchoring` | Three rounds: flush anchoring (PR #27), hiding the dock/rail/fabs behind sheets (PR #28), and finally the real culprit — the 30% backdrop dim itself was the "ghost panel" (PR #29). Sheets now rise over the map with an invisible tap-to-close backdrop. *✦ Shipped — PR #27, #28, #29* | S | **Shipped** (PR #27, #28, #29) |
| **F5** | `feat/departure-semantics` | Leaving marks left_at instead of deleting the row (migration 0004 + on_member_left trigger). Departed members freeze dimmed at their last known position, show "left" in the rail and "Left — last known position" on the card, are excluded from convergence/allArrived, and their trails flow into the archive. *✦ Shipped — PR #30* | M | **Shipped** (PR #30) |
| **F6** | `fix/marker-drops` | The long one — four takes against rn-maps #5911 (Apple Maps + New Arch loses custom marker views on ANY lifecycle event). Removed opacity flips and selection-driven regrouping (#31), removed the -sel remount "self-heal" that caused a 3-5s blink on every Close (#32), then killed churn entirely: live sessions skip clustering (pucks mount once at roster load, never unmount or hide) and a 5s repaintTick prop nudges any natively-lost view back into existence. *✦ Shipped — PR #31, #32 — verified on device. Facepiles remain demo-only.* | L | **Shipped** (PR #31, #32) |
| **F7** | `ui/uniform-member-surface` | Rail chips and the full-width card share one fixed height (MEMBER_SURFACE_H), so no member state — departed, arrived, long name — changes the footprint; every line truncates instead of wrapping. ETA reads m:ss → "1:15 hr" past an hour → "5.1 days" past a day. Surfaces scaled +10% after device feedback. *✦ Shipped — PR #34* | M | **Shipped** (PR #34) |
| **F8** | `feat/member-pager` | The detail view is a paging card deck: swipe left/right between members, each landing focuses that member (camera follows). Order is you first, then fastest ETA, departed last — shared with the rail via sortMembers. Rail ⇄ deck cross-fades. Deck order freezes while open so live ETA crossings cannot reorder pages mid-swipe. *✦ Shipped — PR #35* | M | **Shipped** (PR #35) |
| **F9** | `ui/dock-flush` | Dock spans edge-to-edge and sits flush on the screen bottom (bottom corners squared, matching the invite sheet). Expanding it springs the member surface fully out of view; collapsing brings it back. *✦ Shipped — PR #36* | S | **Shipped** (PR #36) |
| **F10** | `chore/refactor-tests` | The fresher-wins snapshot merge, broadcast apply, and tracked → SimMember motion mapping were inline in useLiveTrip and untestable without a Supabase channel. Now pure functions (mergeSnapshot, applyBroadcast, simMotion) with tests; MemberCard memoized so the 4 Hz tick stops re-rendering every page of the deck. *✦ Shipped — PR #37* | M | **Shipped** (PR #37) |
| **F11** | `ui/chips-top` | The map controls belong at the top, out of the way of the member deck and the dock at the bottom. *✦ Shipped — PR #38* | S | **Shipped** (PR #38) |
| **F12** | `feat/demo-entry` | The original 6–8 person NYC walk with real photos, on the home screen above the archives — so every change can be exercised end to end without two phones and a walk around the block. *✦ Shipped — PR #39* | S | **Shipped** (PR #39) |
| **F13** | `fix/map-contract` | THE BIG ONE. Pucks vanished, a "big dot splatter" flashed when switching members, the Trails toggle did nothing for most people, and trails went cold mid-walk. Symptom-patching had failed four times, so we read the vendored rn-maps source instead and found the actual mechanics: AIRMap never overrides didUpdateReactSubviews, so ANY change to MapView's child list re-adds EVERY marker annotation (→ MapKit's default red balloon, or the custom view is simply lost); a Polyline's renderer is built ONLY in setCoordinates:, so a strokeColor change never redraws; dash patterns re-rasterize on every zoom change, so re-applying zoom on the tick was shredding the trails. Fixes: static child list (clustering deleted), markers first / trails last, visibility driven by coordinates never colour, a live trail head, zoom applied once on entering follow, and stop pins as a fixed pre-mounted pool (the last dynamic child list on the map, found only after the first fix shipped). Then made permanent: .claude/skills/arrival-map/SKILL.md + src/map-contract.test.ts, which fails the build if any of it comes back. *✦ Shipped — PR #40, #41 — verified on the old-arch dev build. Marker bugs CANNOT be validated in Expo Go (it forces New Arch, where #5911 lives).* | L | **Shipped** (PR #40, #41) |
| **F14** | `feat/session-setup` | Session creation collapsed onto the main page — no separate create screen for the common case. *✦ Shipped — PR #42* | S | **Shipped** (PR #42) |
| **F15** | `feat/free-roam` | Three things people actually do. (1) FREE ROAM: a session with nowhere to be — just track us and keep our trails. etaMin becomes `number ⎮ null`, and null is a first-class state everywhere, not a zero. (2) A DESTINATION YOU CHOOSE LATER: people get in the session first and argue about where to go afterwards, so any member can set or change the destination mid-session and everyone sees it (set_destination RPC + trips joins the realtime publication). The picker is a search field with cards — rating, review count, distance, link out — because a destination is a decision, not a pin. (3) LEAVE, NOT END: leaving is personal (it marks YOUR left_at); it does not end anyone else's session, and you can rejoin with the same code. A session finishes on its own terms — its time runs out, or everyone has left — and archiving follows that, not a button. *✦ Shipped — PR #43. Merged untested at your call ("we are going to revisit this"). ⚠ Migration 0005 is NOT applied to the live project — demo works; a live session cannot set a destination until it is.* | L | **Shipped** (PR #43) |

---

**28 shipped · 3 in progress · 4 blocked · 33 open.**
